rationale: The citing paper gives Simester et al. (2000) a clear functional role in its literature review, describing it as the key prior marketing quasi-experiment that addresses treatment heterogeneity. It then uses that paper's limits (a two-wave design) to motivate its own methodological contribution for multisite, multiwave field interventions. The cited study is also referenced again in the conclusion as a notable prior exception, reinforcing that it is part of the paper's core positioning rather than a passing mention.
relation_description: The 2009 study builds on Simester et al. (2000) as a methodological predecessor in quasi-experimental evaluation of service quality interventions, and extends that line by proposing a more general multisite, multiwave framework with site-specific Bayesian effect estimates.
